entries.label: Basic allocation
entries.code:
let b = Box::new(5);
println!("{b}");
entries.note: Allocates value on the heap; owns the data; auto-derefs

entries.label: Recursive types
entries.note: Box gives recursive types a known size at compile time

entries.label: Trait objects
entries.note: Box<dyn Trait> for heap-allocated dynamic dispatch

entries.label: Box::leak
entries.code: let s: &'static str = Box::leak(String::from("hello").into_boxed_str());
entries.note: Leak memory to get a 'static reference; useful for global data

entries.label: Into and from raw
entries.note: Convert to/from raw pointer; must manually manage lifetime

entries.label: Shared ownership
entries.note: Multiple owners; reference count tracks how many Rc point to data

entries.label: Accessing data
entries.note: Rc auto-derefs to the inner type for method calls

entries.label: Rc is immutable
entries.note: Rc only gives shared (&T) access; combine with RefCell for mutation

entries.label: Rc::try_unwrap
entries.code:
let rc = Rc::new(42);
let val = Rc::try_unwrap(rc).unwrap(); // Ok if count == 1
entries.note: Extract the inner value if this is the only Rc

entries.label: Shared graph node
entries.note: Common pattern for tree/graph structures with shared nodes

entries.label: Thread-safe shared ownership
entries.note: Like Rc but uses atomic operations; safe across threads

entries.label: Arc + Mutex
entries.note: Arc for shared ownership + Mutex for interior mutability across threads

entries.label: Arc vs Rc
entries.note: Use Rc when single-threaded; Arc when crossing thread boundaries

entries.label: Borrow at runtime
entries.note: Enforces borrowing rules at runtime instead of compile time

entries.label: borrow and borrow_mut
entries.note: Cannot have borrow() and borrow_mut() active simultaneously; panics at runtime

entries.label: try_borrow
entries.note: Returns Result instead of panicking on borrow conflict

entries.label: Rc<RefCell<T>>
entries.note: Multiple owners with mutability; common pattern for graphs and shared state

entries.label: Get and set
entries.note: Interior mutability for Copy types; no borrowing, just get/set

entries.label: In structs
entries.note: Allows mutation through &self; useful for caches and counters

entries.label: Cell vs RefCell
entries.note: Prefer Cell for Copy types; RefCell when you need references to inner data

entries.label: Borrowed or owned
entries.note: Avoids cloning when not necessary; borrows when possible, owns when needed

entries.label: From &str or String
entries.note: Cow<str> can hold either &str or String transparently

entries.label: into_owned
entries.code:
let cow: Cow<str> = Cow::Borrowed("hello");
let owned: String = cow.into_owned(); // clones if borrowed
entries.note: Convert to owned type; only clones if currently borrowing

entries.label: Cow with Vec
entries.note: Works with any type implementing ToOwned: str, [T], Path, etc.

entries.label: Prevent reference cycles
entries.note: Weak does not prevent deallocation; breaks reference cycles

entries.label: Create and upgrade
entries.note: downgrade creates Weak; upgrade returns Option<Rc<T>>

entries.label: Weak count
entries.note: Track both strong and weak reference counts

entries.label: Basic Pin
entries.note: Guarantees the value will not be moved in memory

entries.label: Why Pin exists
entries.note: Futures may contain self-references; Pin ensures they stay in place

entries.label: Box::pin
entries.code:
use std::pin::Pin;
let pinned: Pin<Box<MyFuture>> = Box::pin(MyFuture::new());
entries.note: Pin a heap-allocated value; common for futures

entries.label: Unpin trait
entries.note: Unpin means Pin has no effect; most types are Unpin by default
